features.title: Login page
features.description: Route /login - public page for user authentication.

features.title: Logout
features.description:
Route /logout — destroys session and redirects.

Pages:
- GET /logout

Access:
- Any authenticated user
- Unauthenticated: redirect to /login

Functional:
- Clears JWT token from session (jwt_token, user_role, user)
- Invalidates PHP session
- Redirects to /login

Edge cases:
- Calling /logout when not logged in: redirect to /login (no error)
- Session expired: same behavior as unauthenticated
- After logout, navigating to /admin must redirect to /login (no cached page served)

features.title: Account activation page
features.description:
Route /activate?token=xxx — public page for first-time account activation.

Pages:
- GET /activate?token=xxx

Access:
- Public (no auth required)

Functional:
- On load: call GET /api/auth/activation-status?token=xxx
- Valid token: show password creation form (password + confirmation)
- Invalid/expired token: show error message with link to contact admin
- On submit: POST /api/auth/activate with {token, password}
- Success: show success message with link to /login
- Failure: show error message
- Password minimum 6 characters (validated client-side)

UI elements:
- Same full-page centered card layout as login
- Horoazhon branding
- Password field: 'Nouveau mot de passe'
- Confirmation field: 'Confirmer le mot de passe'
- Submit button: 'Activer mon compte'
- Password strength indicator (visual meter showing weak/medium/strong)

Business rules:
- Password confirmation must match (client-side validation)
- Token is single-use: refreshing after activation shows invalid token

Edge cases:
- No token parameter: show error
- Already-used token: show invalid token message
- Password mismatch: client-side error before submission
- Bookmarked URL visited after token expires: clear error message with 'Contactez votre administrateur' guidance

features.title: Password reset flow (forgot + reset pages)
features.description:
Routes /forgot-password and /reset-password?token=xxx — public pages for password recovery.

Pages:
- GET /forgot-password
- GET /reset-password?token=xxx

Access:
- Both pages are public (no auth required)
- Note: no authenticated-user redirect exists — an already-logged-in user can visit these pages without being redirected to /profil

Forgot password page:
- Form with email field
- Submits POST /api/auth/forgot-password via API client
- On success: show confirmation message ('Email envoyé si le compte existe')
- Anti-enumeration: same success message whether email exists or not

Reset password page:
- On load: validate token via GET /api/auth/reset-status?token=xxx
- Valid token: show password + confirmation form
- Invalid/expired token: show error with link to /forgot-password
- On submit: POST /api/auth/reset-password with {token, password}
- Success: show confirmation with link to /login

UI elements:
- Same full-page centered card layout as login/activate
- Forgot: email field 'Adresse e-mail', submit 'Envoyer le lien', back link to /login
- Reset: password fields + submit 'Réinitialiser'
- Success/error messages should use role='alert' for accessibility

Edge cases:
- Reset with expired token: error page with link to /forgot-password
- Reset with already-used token: error page with link to /forgot-password
- Multiple forgot-password requests: each invalidates previous token, only the last link works

features.title: Public homepage
features.description:
Route / — public landing page of the application.

Pages:
- GET /

Access:
- Public (no auth required)
- Authenticated users also see this page (sidebar shows logged-in state)

Functional:
- Hero section with search form for properties
- Search form: type tabs (Acheter/Louer), location, property type, price range
- Featured/recent properties section showing 6 latest listings (page=0, size=6)
- Search redirects to /biens with query parameters (selected tab maps to forSale/forRent param)

UI elements:
- Sidebar with Horoazhon logo, nav links (Accueil, Biens, Agences), login/dashboard button
- Hero: title 'Trouvez votre bien idéal', subtitle, search form with pill tabs
- Property cards: image, price, title, location, badges (type, surface)
- Footer with contact info, links, copyright

Edge cases:
- No properties in database: featured section handles empty gracefully (no broken cards)
- Very long property titles: truncated in cards
- Search tab selection (Acheter/Louer) must carry over correctly to /biens filter params

features.title: Public property listing and detail
features.description:
Routes /biens and /biens/{id} — public pages for browsing properties.

Pages:
- GET /biens (list with filters)
- GET /biens/{id} (detail)

Access:
- Both pages are public (no auth required)

List page:
- Paginated property grid (12 per page) from GET /api/biens
- Filter bar: search text, type (vente/location via annonce param), price range, property type
- Per-characteristic filters: caracMin_{id}, lieuMax_{id}, lieuLoco_{id} query params
- Pagination controls
- Property cards with image, price, title, location, surface, type badge

Detail page:
- Full property info from GET /api/biens/{id}
- Photo gallery with all uploaded images (fallback placeholder if none)
- Property details: price, surface, description, address, eco score
- Characteristics list and proximity locations from API
- Agency info with contact details
- Contracts section visible only for admin/agent roles (hidden for CLIENT and unauthenticated)
- Back to list link

UI elements:
- List: filter bar, result count, responsive grid (3 cols desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile)
- Detail: breadcrumb, image gallery, info sections, agency card

Edge cases:
- No results matching filters: 'Aucun bien trouvé' message
- Nonexistent property ID: 404 page or redirect
- Property with no photos: placeholder image (no broken img tag)
- Pagination: .pagination div only renders when totalPages > 1 (conditional: {% if totalPages > 1 %}). With V2 seed data (10 properties) and default size=12, pagination is hidden because all results fit on one page. Tests must use ?size=2 or similar to force multiple pages before asserting .pagination exists.
- Multiple filters simultaneously: AND logic works correctly (type + price + annonce)
- Responsive: grid adapts from 3 → 2 → 1 columns

features.title: Public agency listing and detail
features.description:
Routes /agences and /agences/{id} — public pages for browsing agencies.

Pages:
- GET /agences (list)
- GET /agences/{id} (detail with agency properties)

Access:
- Both pages are public (no auth required)

List page:
- Controller: AgencePublicController with agences_list route
- Template: templates/agence/list.html.twig
- All agencies from GET /api/agences
- Agency cards in grid layout with logo, name, address, contact info
- Each card links to /agences/{id}
- Agency with no logo: placeholder image
- Responsive: 3 cols desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile

Detail page:
- Agency info from GET /api/agences/{id}
- Agency properties from GET /api/agences/{id}/biens (paginated, 12 per page)
- Contact information section
- Agency with no logo: placeholder image
- Properties MUST be visible for unauthenticated users (backend SecurityConfig now permits GET /api/agences/*/biens)
- Frontend note: AgencePublicController.php has a try/catch (lines 31-42) that falls back to empty $biens on API error — this is safe fallback behavior, not a bug

UI elements:
- List: agency cards in grid layout, consistent with property card style
- Detail: agency header (logo, name, address), property listing, contact info

Edge cases:
- Agency with no properties: show agency info, empty property section with message
- Nonexistent agency ID: 404 page or redirect
- Agency with no logo: placeholder image
- No agencies in database: empty state message
- Unauthenticated user on detail page: must see properties (previously broken, now fixed)

features.title: Base layout (navbar, footer, responsive)
features.description:
base.html.twig — shared layout inherited by all pages.

Components:
- Fixed 240px sidebar (left), main content area (right)
- Footer inside main content area
- Flash messages: success/error/info alerts with dismiss buttons
- Responsive breakpoints

Sidebar structure:
- Brand header: Horoazhon logo + name
- Agency badge (if user belongs to an agency with logo)
- Role-adaptive navigation sections with active link highlighting (border-left: 3px solid #2563eb)
- User footer: avatar initial, name, role label, logout button
- Login button for unauthenticated users

Sidebar sections by role:
- Unauthenticated: Accueil, Biens, Agences + 'Se connecter' button
- CLIENT: + Tableau de bord, Mes contrats, Mes biens, Mon profil
- AGENT: + Admin section (Tableau de bord, Biens, Contrats, Personnes, Mon profil)
- ADMIN_AGENCY: + Utilisateurs, Paramètres agence
- SUPER_ADMIN: + Agences, Données de référence

Active link detection: Twig route matching with 'starts with' for prefix groups

Footer:
- '© {year} Horoazhon. Tous droits réservés.'
- Navigation links

Responsive:
- Desktop (>=768px): sidebar always visible, main content offset
- Mobile (<768px): sidebar hidden, hamburger toggle (fixed top-left), slide-out drawer with dark overlay, Escape key closes, body scroll locked when open

Edge cases:
- Very long user name in sidebar footer: must truncate (verify ellipsis)
- Very long agency name in sidebar badge: must wrap or truncate
- Flash messages: dismissible with animation, stacked vertically if multiple
- Hamburger button must have aria-label, sidebar should use <nav> landmark
- Auth pages override sidebar/footer visibility (full-page centered card)

features.title: Admin dashboard
features.description:
Route /admin — main dashboard for administrative users.

Pages:
- GET /admin

Access:
- SUPER_ADMIN: full dashboard with all stats
- ADMIN_AGENCY: dashboard scoped to own agency
- AGENT: dashboard scoped to own agency
- CLIENT: redirect to /client
- Unauthenticated: redirect to /login

Functional:
- Statistics cards: biens count, agences count, personnes count, contrats count
- Data fetching: biens and contrats use page=0, size=1 for totalElements counts; agences and personnes load ALL records and use count() (performance concern for large datasets)
- Stat cards with colored left borders (4 color variants)
- Quick access links to admin sections
- Sidebar navigation to all admin sections (inherited from base.html.twig)

UI elements:
- Stat cards in grid layout with colored left borders
- Quick access links as buttons/cards
- No recent activity tables (stat cards only for now)

Edge cases:
- New agency with zero data: all stat cards show 0, not errors
- SUPER_ADMIN with multiple agencies: aggregate stats
- Verify totalElements from size=1 queries is accurate

features.title: Admin property pages (list, create, edit, archive)
features.description:
Routes /admin/biens, /admin/biens/new, /admin/biens/{id}/edit — property CRUD and archiving for admin users.

Pages:
- GET /admin/biens (list)
- GET /admin/biens/new (create form)
- POST /admin/biens/new (create submit)
- GET /admin/biens/{id}/edit (edit form with multi-tab management)
- POST /admin/biens/{id}/edit (edit submit — uses _action parameter for routing)
- POST /admin/biens/{id}/delete (delete action)
- POST /admin/biens/{id}/archive (archive action)
- POST /admin/biens/{id}/unarchive (unarchive action)

Access:
- SUPER_ADMIN: all operations on all properties
- ADMIN_AGENCY: all operations on own agency properties
- AGENT: read own agency, create, edit own properties
- CLIENT: can only edit properties they own
- Unauthenticated: redirect to /login

List page:
- Table with summary stat cards at top
- Search/filter bar
- Filter tabs: Actifs / Archives / Tous (sends ?actif= parameter to backend)
- Pagination (20 per page)
- Action buttons: view, edit, archive/unarchive, delete (based on permissions)
- Archive status badge on each property row (actif=false shows 'Archive' badge)
- 'Ajouter un bien' button

Create form:
- Fields: basic property info + agency dropdown (SUPER_ADMIN only)
- Agency auto-assigned for non-SUPER_ADMIN from session
- Two-step: create bien first, then redirect to edit page for sub-resources

Edit form (multi-tab):
- Tab 1: Info (rue, ville, codePostal, type, superficie, ecoScore, description)
- Tab 2: Sales listing (Achat — prix, dateDispo) — inline add/edit/delete
- Tab 3: Rental listing (Location — caution, mensualite, dureeMois, dateDispo) — inline add/edit/delete
- Tab 4: Characteristics — add/remove from reference list (unite, valeur)
- Tab 5: Proximity — add/remove lieux (minutes, typeLocomotion)
- Tab 6: Owner — assign/remove proprietaire from personne dropdown
- Tab 7: Photos — upload/remove (stored to /public/uploads/photos)
- Uses _action POST parameter to route to correct handler per tab
- Archived property: shows archive banner with 'Reactiver' button. Listing tabs (Achat/Location) disabled for archived properties.

Archive/Unarchive:
- Archive calls PUT /api/biens/{id}/archive — guards: fails if SIGNE contract exists (409). Side-effects: deletes offers, cancels EN_COURS contracts, sets actif=false.
- Unarchive calls PUT /api/biens/{id}/unarchive — sets actif=true, no side-effects.
- BienDTO includes actif boolean field for all display logic.

Delete behavior:
- DELETE /api/biens/{id} returns 409 if property has any contracts. Error message suggests archiving instead ('Ce bien a des contrats — veuillez l'archiver').
- Hard delete only succeeds if property has zero contracts ever.

Business rules:
- ADMIN_AGENCY/AGENT: agency auto-assigned from session
- SUPER_ADMIN: must select agency from dropdown
- Photos: JPEG/PNG, auto-generated filenames, stored in /public/uploads/photos

Edge cases:
- No properties: 'Aucun bien trouve' message with add button
- Delete confirmation dialog
- Delete property with contracts: 409 with archive suggestion
- Archive property with SIGNE contract: 409 error shown
- Form validation errors: fields highlighted, messages shown
- Multi-tab _action routing: each tab action must work independently
- Create -> immediate redirect to edit page with tabs must work cleanly

features.title: Admin agency pages (list, create, edit, settings)
features.description:
Routes /admin/agences, /admin/agences/new, /admin/agences/{id}/edit, /admin/agences/settings — agency management.

Pages:
- GET /admin/agences (list)
- GET /admin/agences/new (create form, SUPER_ADMIN only)
- POST /admin/agences/new (create submit)
- GET /admin/agences/{id}/edit (edit form)
- POST /admin/agences/{id}/edit (edit submit)
- POST /admin/agences/{id}/delete (delete action, SUPER_ADMIN only)
- GET /admin/agences/settings (agency settings — reads agency ID from session, no {id} in URL)

Access:
- SUPER_ADMIN: all operations on all agencies
- ADMIN_AGENCY: edit own agency, view settings for own agency (no create, no delete, no other agencies)
- AGENT: read only (no forms, no actions)
- CLIENT: no access
- Unauthenticated: redirect to /login

features.title: Admin person pages (list, create, edit)
features.description:
Routes /admin/personnes, /admin/personnes/new, /admin/personnes/{id}/edit — person management.

Pages:
- GET /admin/personnes (list)
- GET /admin/personnes/search.json?q=... (JSON autocomplete endpoint)
- GET /admin/personnes/new (create form)
- POST /admin/personnes/new (create submit)
- GET /admin/personnes/{id}/edit (edit form)
- POST /admin/personnes/{id}/edit (edit submit)
- POST /admin/personnes/{id}/invite (send activation email)
- POST /admin/personnes/{id}/delete (delete action)

Access:
- SUPER_ADMIN: all operations
- ADMIN_AGENCY: all operations
- AGENT: all operations
- CLIENT: no access
- Unauthenticated: redirect to /login

List page:
- Table: nom, prenom, email, telephone columns
- Shows ALL personnes globally (no agency filtering — Personne is agency-agnostic)
- Search by name/email (accent-insensitive, global)
- Pagination (20 per page) for default listing. Search results are unpaginated.
- Action buttons: edit, delete, invite

JSON search endpoint:
- /admin/personnes/search.json?q=... for autocomplete in contract creation
- Requires q >= 1 character
- Accent-insensitive, returns all matching personnes globally

Form page:
- Fields: nom, prenom, dateNais, rue, ville, codePostal, rib

Edit page enrichments:
- Shows owned properties (from getPersonneBiens)
- Shows signed contracts (from getPersonneContrats)
- Shows account status (from getPersonneAccountStatus): hasAccount, status (ACTIVE/PENDING/EXPIRED), email
- Invite button: sends activation email, shows /activate?token=X in flash message
- Invite use case: admin finds a person from another agency, invites them to join their own agency

Business rules:
- Personnes linked to active contracts cannot be deleted
- Account status check is optional (gracefully skips if endpoint fails)

Edge cases:
- No personnes: empty table with add button
- Delete personne with contracts: error message explaining why blocked
- Search with accents/special characters: verify accent-insensitive matching works
- Invite to already-activated person: verify appropriate feedback
- Person from another agency visible in list: expected behavior (global list)

features.title: Admin user pages (list, create, edit)
features.description:
Routes /admin/utilisateurs, /admin/utilisateurs/new - user account management.

Pages:
- GET /admin/utilisateurs (list)
- GET /admin/utilisateurs/new (create/invite form)
- POST /admin/utilisateurs/new (invite submit)
- POST /admin/utilisateurs/{id}/deactivate (soft-disable)
- POST /admin/utilisateurs/{id}/reactivate (re-enable)

Note: No edit form for existing users - only create, deactivate, reactivate.

Access:
- SUPER_ADMIN: all operations on all users
- ADMIN_AGENCY: manage users in own agency (create AGENT/CLIENT only)
- AGENT: can view user list, can manage CLIENT users (create, deactivate, reactivate). Controller has no role guard — template conditionally shows actions.
- CLIENT: no access
- Unauthenticated: redirect to /login

List page:
- Table (first page only, no pagination controls): email, nom, prenom, role (badge), status (actif/inactif). API returns page 0 with size 20 but no UI to navigate to subsequent pages.
- Deactivate/reactivate action buttons per user
- 'Inviter un utilisateur' button

Create/invite form:
- Fields: email, nom, prenom, dateNais, role (dropdown)
- SUPER_ADMIN: can assign agenceId via dropdown
- ADMIN_AGENCY: agency auto-assigned, role limited to AGENT and CLIENT
- On submit: creates Personne + Compte with activation token
- Activation URL shown in flash message (needs 'Copy' button for usability)

Business rules:
- Email must be unique (400 on duplicate)
- Role hierarchy: ADMIN_AGENCY cannot create ADMIN_AGENCY or SUPER_ADMIN accounts (403)
- Deactivation is soft (sets actif=false), reversible via reactivate
- Cannot deactivate the last SUPER_ADMIN

Edge cases:
- No users: empty table
- Invite with existing email: error message shown
- Role hierarchy enforcement: ADMIN_AGENCY attempting to create equal/higher role gets 403
- Activation URL in flash: must remain visible long enough to copy (or provide copy-to-clipboard button)
- Deactivating self: should be forbidden

features.title: Admin reference data page
features.description:
Route /admin/references — manage characteristics and lieux reference data.

Pages:
- GET /admin/references

Access:
- SUPER_ADMIN: full CRUD on both caracteristiques and lieux
- ADMIN_AGENCY, AGENT: read-only view (no add/edit/delete forms visible)
- CLIENT: redirect to /client
- Unauthenticated: redirect to /login

Functional:
- Two-column grid layout: Caracteristiques on left, Lieux on right
- Each section has: list of items, inline add form, edit/delete actions per item
- Add form: single 'lib' text input + 'Ajouter' submit button
- Edit: inline edit with save/cancel
- Delete: confirmation before removal
- Uses GET /api/caracteristiques and GET /api/lieux (public endpoints)
- Create/Update/Delete use POST/PUT/DELETE on respective endpoints (SUPER_ADMIN only)

UI elements:
- .detail-grid layout, .card sections
- Input fields with name='lib'
- 'Ajouter' buttons for each section

Edge cases:
- Empty reference lists: show empty state, not error
- Duplicate lib value: backend may reject with 409
- Non-SUPER_ADMIN trying to add/edit/delete: backend returns 403

features.title: User profile page
features.description:
Route /profil — authenticated user's profile and settings.

Pages:
- GET /profil
- POST /profil (update personal info)
- POST /profil/change-password (change password)

Access:
- Any authenticated user
- Unauthenticated: redirect to /login
- User without personneId in session: redirect to dashboard (CLIENT → /client, others → /admin)

Functional:
- Section 1 — Account info (read-only display): email, role, agency name
- Section 2 — Personal info (editable): nom, prenom, dateNais, rue, ville, codePostal, rib
- Section 3 — Password change: current password + new password + confirmation
- Session sync: updates nom/prenom in session after save (sidebar reflects changes)

UI elements:
- Account info card showing email, role badge, agency name (all read-only)
- Edit form for personal details (Personne fields)
- Separate password change section with three fields
- Save buttons per section

Business rules:
- Email cannot be changed (display only)
- Role and agency cannot be changed by user (display only)
- Current password required for password change (BCrypt verified on backend)
- New password minimum 8 characters (controller checks mb_strlen < 8), confirmation must match

Edge cases:
- Wrong current password: error message from backend
- Password change success: show confirmation flash, stay on page
- No changes submitted: no error, no update
- User without personneId: redirect to dashboard instead of error page
- Session sync after profile update: sidebar must show updated name

features.title: Client dashboard
features.description:
Routes /client, /client/contrats, /client/biens — dashboard for CLIENT role users.

Pages:
- GET /client (dashboard with summary stats)
- GET /client/contrats (paginated contracts list)
- GET /client/biens (paginated properties list)

Access:
- CLIENT: full access
- SUPER_ADMIN/ADMIN_AGENCY/AGENT: currently get 403 (see note below)
- Unauthenticated: redirect to /login

Functional:
- Dashboard: welcome message with user name, summary stats (totalProperties, totalContracts, activeContracts, totalRevenue, monthlyRevenue)
- Contracts page: paginated list (10 per page) of client's own contracts with status badges
- Properties page: paginated list (10 per page) of client's associated properties
- Uses dedicated /api/client/* endpoints (not generic endpoints with filters)
- Graceful error handling: flash message on API failure, empty data fallback

UI elements:
- Uses same sidebar layout as all pages
- Dashboard stat cards, contract/property tables
- Navigation between dashboard/contrats/biens via sidebar links

Business rules:
- Client sees only own contracts (filtered by personneId from JWT on server side)
- No edit/delete capabilities on contracts (read-only)
- Revenue calculated from signed contracts

Test account: client@horoazhon.fr / Client / CLIENT role / agency 1 / personne 14 — exists in V2 seed data.

Edge cases:
- New client with zero contracts/properties: show empty state messages, not errors
- Expired contracts: shown with status badge
- Client not linked to any personne: empty dashboard with message
